title: grevillea juniperina subsp. amphitricha
intro: Grevillea juniperina subsp. amphitricha is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae, native to Western Australia. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in traditional medicine. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Grevillea juniperina subsp. amphitricha include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
